description: SAML 2.0 identity provider integration enabling users to authenticate via a federated identity provider without maintaining local passwords.

actors.name: End User
actors.description: Initiates login and is redirected to the identity provider

actors.name: Identity Provider
actors.description: External SAML IdP that authenticates users and issues assertions

actors.name: System Administrator
actors.description: Configures SAML settings and certificate management

fields.name: idp_metadata_url
fields.label: URL of the identity provider's SAML metadata XML endpoint

fields.name: idp_certificate
fields.label: Public certificate of the identity provider for signature verification

fields.name: service_provider_certificate
fields.label: Service provider public certificate sent to IdP for encryption

fields.name: service_provider_private_key
fields.label: Service provider private key used to decrypt SAML assertions

fields.name: attribute_email
fields.label: SAML assertion attribute name that maps to user email

fields.name: attribute_username
fields.label: SAML assertion attribute name that maps to username

fields.name: attribute_first_name
fields.label: SAML assertion attribute name that maps to first name

fields.name: attribute_last_name
fields.label: SAML assertion attribute name that maps to last name

fields.name: enable_sync_with_ldap
fields.label: When true, SAML user profiles are also synced against an LDAP directory

fields.name: relay_state
fields.label: Opaque token generated per authentication request to prevent CSRF in the SAML fl

values.name: redirect_pending
values.description: Authentication request generated; user redirected to identity provider

values.name: assertion_received
values.description: IdP has posted a SAML assertion back to the service provider

values.name: failed
values.description: Assertion invalid, signature check failed, or attribute mapping error

transitions.description: Identity provider posts SAML response to the assertion consumer service URL
Assertion signature and attributes verified; user session created
Signature invalid, certificate expired, or required attributes missing

rules.rule_01: Users authenticated via SAML do not have or use a local password; password authentication is bypassed.
rules.rule_02: User profile attributes (email, username, first name, last name) are populated from SAML assertion attributes on each login.
rules.rule_03: A relay_state token is generated per authentication request and validated on callback to prevent CSRF attacks.
rules.rule_04: SAML users cannot activate TOTP multi-factor authentication; MFA is delegated to the identity provider.
rules.rule_05: Sessions created from SAML logins are marked as SSO sessions and may have a distinct session length configured separately from password-based sessions.
rules.rule_06: When LDAP sync is enabled alongside SAML, user attributes are additionally reconciled against the LDAP directory after assertion validation.
rules.rule_07: IdP metadata (certificates, endpoints) can be fetched automatically from a metadata URL or uploaded manually.
rules.rule_08: Certificate rotation requires updating both the IdP certificate in configuration and the service provider certificate exchange with the IdP.

given: SAML is enabled in system configuration
user initiated login and was redirected to the identity provider
identity provider posted a valid signed assertion with required attributes
relay_state token matches the pending authentication request

then.description: SSO session created with SAML identity marker and configured SSO session length

sso_login_success.result: User is authenticated and redirected to the application

given: signature verification against IdP certificate fails
sso_login_invalid_signature.result: Login rejected; user sees authentication error

given: required attribute mappings (email or username) are absent in the assertion
sso_login_missing_attributes.result: Login rejected; administrator must fix attribute mapping configuration

given: relay_state does not match any pending authentication request
sso_login_relay_state_invalid.result: Login rejected to prevent CSRF replay attack

given: user authenticated via SAML attempts to enable TOTP MFA
mfa_not_available_for_saml.result: MFA activation blocked; user must configure MFA at the identity provider level

errors.message: Authentication failed. Please contact your administrator.
Authentication failed due to a configuration error. Please contact your administrator.
Authentication request is invalid or has expired. Please try again.
Single sign-on is not configured on this server.
Multi-factor authentication is managed by your identity provider.
Identity provider certificate is missing or invalid.

events.name: auth.saml_login_success
events.description: User successfully authenticated via SAML SSO

events.name: auth.saml_user_provisioned
events.description: New user account created from SAML assertion on first login

related.reason: SSO login produces a session with SSO-specific lifecycle settings
SAML can coexist with LDAP sync for attribute enrichment
MFA is not applicable for SAML users; delegated to the IdP

goals.description: SAML 2.0 identity provider integration enabling users to authenticate via a federated identity provider without maintaining local passwords.

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: authentication must prioritize preventing unauthorized access

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
state transitions follow the defined state machine — no illegal transitions
